compiled_state.title: As a developer, the complexity evaluator identifies when an epic involves external integrations
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add pattern_familiarity (high/medium/low), external_boundary_count (integer), and feasibility_review_recommended (boolean) to the complexity evaluator agent output.
**Why**: Provides a trigger signal for the brainstorm feasibility reviewer. Currently the complexity evaluator classifies SIMPLE/MODERATE/COMPLEX but does not distinguish integration-heavy epics from internally complex ones.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add 3 new output fields to complexity-evaluator agent definition, scoring logic for each field, backward-compatible JSON output
- OUT: Brainstorm integration (separate story), routing threshold changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, complexity evaluator output includes pattern_familiarity, external_boundary_count, and feasibility_review_recommended fields alongside existing classification ← Satisfies SC4
- When this story is complete, existing classification and routing behavior is unchanged — all current consumers produce identical results ← Satisfies SC4
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Output schema consumed by brainstorm — field additions must be backward-compatible
- [Maintainability] The complexity evaluator output schema is consumed by brainstorm, sprint, implementation-plan, and fix-bug. New fields must not break JSON parsing in any consumer. Verify all consumers tolerate unknown fields before merging.

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks completed and committed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
